Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.springboot

Class SpringEnvironmentPropertySource

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.server.springboot.SpringEnvironmentPropertySource
All Implemented Interfaces:
PropertySource
public class SpringEnvironmentPropertySource extends Object implements PropertySource
Bridges a Spring Environment into the Juneau Settings singleton as a PropertySource.

Allows @Value("{spring.property.key}") resolution against the same property sources that Spring Boot would resolve through @org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value: application.yaml, application.properties, command-line args, environment variables, and any custom org.springframework.core.env.PropertySource that Spring loads.

Lifecycle:

Instances are auto-registered by SpringBeanStore's constructor and removed from Settings when SpringBeanStore.clear() is called. Callers that want explicit lifecycle control can construct the source directly and add it via Settings.get().addSource(...).

Lazy resolution:

The Environment is resolved lazily on the first get(String) call so that constructors that build the source from an ApplicationContext do not interact with the context until a lookup actually happens. This matters for tests that mock the context and assert "no interactions" until a Spring-side bean is requested.

Ordering:

Because Settings walks property sources in reverse insertion order, the Spring Environment bridge — registered when Spring creates the bean store — shadows any classpath-default juneau.cfg loaded earlier through the SPI-discovered ConfigPropertySourceProvider. Programmatic addSource() calls made after the Spring context is built (e.g. from a user-provided microservice Config) still win.

See Also:
  • @Value basics
  • SpringBeanStore

  • Constructor Details

    • SpringEnvironmentPropertySource

      public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(org.springframework.core.env.Environment env)
      Constructor that bridges a pre-built Environment.
      Parameters:
      env - The Spring environment to bridge. Must not be null.

    • SpringEnvironmentPropertySource

      public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(Supplier<org.springframework.core.env.Environment> envSupplier)
      Constructor that lazily resolves the Environment on first lookup.

      Use this overload when wiring through ApplicationContext: pass appContext::getEnvironment so the application context isn't invoked until a lookup actually occurs.

      Parameters:
      envSupplier - Supplier of the Spring environment. Must not be null. May return null, in which case this source returns PropertyLookupResult.missing() for every key.

  • Method Details

    • get

      public PropertyLookupResult get(String name)
      Description copied from interface: PropertySource
      Returns a property in this property source.
      Specified by:
      get in interface PropertySource
      Parameters:
      name - The property name.
      Returns:
      The property lookup result:
      • PropertyLookupResult.missing() if this source does not define the key.
      • PropertyLookupResult.present(Optional) with Optional.empty() if the key exists with a null value.
      • PropertyLookupResult.present(Optional) with a value if the key exists with a non-null value.
